title: senecio squarrosus
intro: Senecio squarrosus, also known as Rough-leaved Ragwort, is a perennial plant native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its hairy, greyish-green leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Senecio squarrosus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a natural expectorant, making it a valuable herb for respiratory health. Senecio squarrosus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to potential side effects.
